column1: # ZAP Baseline Scan Rules — SeventySix DAST Configuration

column3: Vulnerable JS Library (false positive — all JS is self-hosted, content-hashed with SRI)
Re-examine Cache-control Directives (intentional: no-cache for HTML, hashed assets cached)
X-Content-Type-Options (already set via nginx/Caddy — ZAP may not see through proxy)
Content Security Policy (intentional strict CSP — reviewed in pen test)
Cross-Domain Misconfiguration (CORS is intentionally strict, same-origin only)
CSP: script-src unsafe-inline (Angular requires unsafe-inline in compiled output — same concern already reviewed under rule 10038)
Timestamp Disclosure - Unix (false positive — Unix timestamps in ngsw-worker.js are Angular service worker cache keys, not sensitive data)
Dangerous JS Functions (false positive — ZAP flags standard Angular/zone.js minified patterns; no eval() in application code)
Loosely Scoped Cookie (SameSite=Strict already set on auth cookies)
